title: Spin-orbit coupling effects in altermagnets: Interplay of weak spin and orbital ferromagnetism with relativistic splitting of electron states
authors: L. M. Sandratskii, K. Carva, V. M. Silkin
abstract: The aim of the paper is to contribute to reaching a deeper understanding of the formation of relativistic effects in altermagnets. The focus of the paper is on the phenomena of weak ferromagnetism (WFM) and relativistic DFT calculations combined with the symmetry analysis on the basis of spin space groups. The consideration is performed on two different levels. On the first level, the atomistic magnetic structure of weak ferromagnetic state is calculated. Both spin and orbital atomic moments are taken into account. We study the dependence of the WFM moment on the strength of the SOC and obtain a peculiar nonmonotonous type of dependence. An interesting result is obtained in quasisymmetry (QS) calculation where only the component of the SOC collinear to the Néel vector is taken into account. In QS calculation the spin WFM is absent while the orbital WFM is present. This reveals a principal difference in the formation of the spin and orbital magnetic moments. On the second level, the study is focused on the properties of individual electron states. We introduce the notion of the magnetic structure of the electron state (MSES). It is shown how the collinear spin-MSESs of both metal and ligand atoms and compensated orbital-MSES of the ligand obtained in the nonrelativiatic calculation transform into complex noncollinear 3D MSESs of both spin and orbital nature. An important role in the formation of MSESs is played by the relativistic splitting of the accidental spin degeneracies at general {\bf k} points filling the volume of the Brillouin zone. The formation of the regions of avoided crossings in the relativistic band structure is related to the nonmonotonous behavior of the WFM moment. The importance of the metal-ligand hybridization in the formation of the AM properties is discussed. Most of the calculations are performed for MnTe.
summary: This paper explores how spin-orbit coupling induces weak ferromagnetism and complex 3D magnetic structures in altermagnets like MnTe. It shows that the relativistic splitting of electron states and the formation of avoided crossings are central to the nonmonotonous behavior of induced magnetic moments. The work highlights the critical role of metal-ligand hybridization in defining altermagnetic properties.
main_problem: Investigating how spin-orbit coupling (SOC) drives relativistic effects in altermagnets, specifically the interplay between weak spin/orbital ferromagnetism and the relativistic splitting of electron states.
main_result: The study reveals a nonmonotonous dependence of the weak ferromagnetic (WFM) moment on SOC strength and demonstrates that SOC transforms collinear spin-MSES into complex 3D noncollinear magnetic structures through avoided crossings.
method: Relativistic Density Functional Theory (DFT) using the Augmented Spherical Waves (ASW) method, incorporating LDA, GGA, and LDA+U, combined with symmetry analysis based on spin-space groups.
model_or_system: The prototypical altermagnet MnTe (NiAs crystal lattice) and CrSb, focusing on the transition from nonrelativistic to relativistic regimes.
key_observables: Spin and orbital atomic magnetic moments, weak ferromagnetic (WFM) moments, and the Magnetic Structure of Electron States (MSES).
important_parameters: SOC strength (scaled by gamma), Néel vector orientation, Hubbard U parameter, and metal-ligand hybridization.
main_assumptions: The local atomic z-axis is assumed to be parallel to the direction of the atomic spin moment in the SOC operator; use of the quasisymmetry approximation to decouple spin and orbital components.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the MnTe unit cell and magnetic canting; Figures 2-6 illustrate the dependence of spin/orbital moments on SOC strength; Figures 10-12 and 17-20 show the evolution of MSES and band structure features like avoided crossings.
paper_structure: The paper progresses from an atomistic analysis of magnetic structures and WFM to an electronic-level study of electron state magnetic structures (MSES), utilizing symmetry hierarchies (NR to QS to Relativistic) to explain the emergence of complex 3D magnetism.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1: (a) Crystallographic and magnetic unit cell of MnTe. bf L is the N´eel vector of the magnetic structure. In the paper, the x axis is selected parallel to the N´eel vector. (b) Canting of the Mn atomic moments of the two sublattices leading to weak ferromagnetism.

FIG. 2: Induced moments as functions of the SOC strength. LDA calculation for MnTe. L||x. (a) The component of the Mn orbital moment collinear to the N´eel vector. (b)-(e) The components of the Mn spin (b) and orbital (c) and Te spin (d) and orbital (e) moments collinear to the z axis and con- tributing to WFM. These components are multiplied by 103. In all graphs the results of the calculations with SOC on Mn atoms only are shown in red, with SOC on Te atoms only are in blau, and with SOC on both atoms are in black.
FIG. 3: The same as in Fig. 2 but calculated with LDA+U method.

FIG. 6: Induced moments as functions of the SOC strength. GGA quasisymmetry calculation for CrSb. (a) The compo- nent of the Cr orbital moment collinear to the N´eel vector. (b) and (e) The components of the Cr and Sb orbital moments collinear to the z axis and contributing to WFM. These com- ponents are multiplied by 103. Colors of the curves are the same as in in Fig. 2

FIG. 4: Induced moments as functions of the SOC strength. LDA+U quasisymmetry calculation for MnTe. (a) The com- ponent of the Mn orbital moment collinear to the N´eel vec- tor. (b) and (e) The components of the Mn and Te orbital moments collinear to the z axis and contributing to WFM. These components are multiplied by 103. Colors of the curves are the same as in Fig. 2.
